summary: Web-research + spec the View tab gaps

specs.feature: View tab — view modes and zoom: Outline view, Draft view, Immersive Reader, and the 100% zoom button. In ribbon-data.js these are controls `view.views.outline` (cmd `outline`), `view.views.draft` (cmd `draft`), `view.immersive.immersive-reader` (cmd `immersiveReader`), and `view.zoom.100` (cmd `"100"`). Read Mode/Print Layout/Web Layout already work via `WC.Editor.setView('read'|'print'|'web')`; Zoom dialog, One Page, Multiple Pages, Page Width, Focus, Ruler, Gridlines, Navigation Pane already work. The four targets here currently have NO command handlers, so `WC.Commands.run` (commands.js:424) falls through to `WC.notImplemented(...)`.
specs.wordBehavior:
OUTLINE VIEW (real Word): Switching to Outline opens a dedicated "Outlining" ribbon tab with an "Outline Tools" group. The body renders as an indented tree keyed off built-in Heading 1–9 / Body Text outline levels (Heading 1 flush left, lower levels indented further; non-heading paragraphs render as "Body Text"). Each heading shows an outline symbol: a circled plus/minus (expand/collapse) and a small dot for body text. Tools: Promote (one level / move left), Promote to Heading 1 (double arrow), Demote (one level / move right), Demote to Body Text (double arrow), Move Up / Move Down (reorder the selected heading WITH its subordinate content), Expand / Collapse (the +/- collapses a heading's children & body text to a single line), "Show Level" dropdown (Level 1..9 / All Levels — picking Level 3 shows Levels 1–3 only and hides deeper), "Show Text Formatting" checkbox (off = plain uniform font), "Show First Line Only" checkbox (long body paragraphs collapse to first line + "…"). Promote/demote actually re-tags the paragraph's heading level in the underlying document. No page boundaries, headers/footers, images or columns are drawn.

DRAFT VIEW (real Word): A simplified continuous writing view. NO top/bottom page margins, NO page edges/shadow — pages run together with manual/automatic page breaks shown only as thin dotted horizontal lines. Does NOT render headers, footers, page background/watermark, floating images, multi-column layout, or drawing objects. Optimized for fast text entry. Essentially Web Layout but plainer (single fixed-width column) with dotted page-break markers retained.

IMMERSIVE READER (real Word): A distraction-free full-window reading experience launched from View > Immersive Reader, with its own toolbar. Controls: Column Width (Very Narrow / Narrow / Moderate / Wide — line measure); Page Color (palette incl. white/sepia/black-inverse + ~12 colors, plus More Colors); Line Focus (off / 1 / 3 / 5 lines — dims everything except a focused band, move line-by-line); Text Spacing (toggle — wider spacing between words/characters/lines); Syllables (toggle — visible separators between syllables, e.g. "im·mer·sive"); Read Aloud (TTS, highlights each word as it reads, with voice selection + reading-speed control). Closing returns to the prior view.

100% BUTTON (real Word): Instantly sets zoom to exactly 100% (1.0). One-click, no dialog.
specs.webFindings:
All four facts confirmed via web search of Microsoft Support and how-to guides:
- Outline view tools & behavior — Microsoft Support "Collapse or expand parts of a document"; howtogeek "How to Use Outline View in Microsoft Word"; dummies "Word 2019 Outline View": confirm Outlining tab, Outline Tools group, Promote/Demote (single + to-Level-1 / to-Body-Text double arrows), Move Up/Down (moves selection with subordinates), Expand/Collapse via +/- symbols, "Show Level" dropdown (Level N shows 1..N), "Show Text Formatting", "Show First Line Only". https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/office/collapse-or-expand-parts-of-a-document-701786e0-95e2-40bf-bfe5-f0233cd3520c , https://www.howtogeek.com/726748/how-to-use-outline-view-in-microsoft-word/ , https://www.dummies.com/article/technology/software/microsoft-products/word/how-writers-can-use-word-2019s-outline-view-259128/
- Draft view — dummies "Word Document Views"; wordribbon.tips.net "Understanding Views"; Noble Desktop "Exploring Different Document Views": Draft shows content without top/bottom margins, page breaks as dotted lines, hides images/headers/footers, simplified for fast editing. https://www.dummies.com/article/technology/software/microsoft-products/general-microsoft/word-document-views-and-how-to-switch-between-them-257308/ , https://wordribbon.tips.net/T008142_Understanding_Views.html
- Immersive Reader — Microsoft Support "Use Immersive Reader in Word"; groovypost: Column Width (Very Narrow/Narrow/Moderate/Wide), Page Color (12+ colors + More Colors), Line Focus (1/3/5 lines), Text Spacing, Syllables, Read Aloud (per-word highlight, voice + speed). https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/office/use-immersive-reader-in-word-a857949f-c91e-4c97-977c-a4efcaf9b3c1 , https://www.groovypost.com/howto/enable-and-customize-immersive-reader-in-microsoft-word/
- 100% button — confirmed by Microsoft tooltip already in ribbon-data.js ("Zoom the document to 100% of the normal size").
specs.implementationPlan:
All work is in existing files plus two new self-registering WC modules. Reuse: `WC.Editor.setView/setZoom/repaginate/node/selectedBlocks/getHTML`, `WC.el`, `WC.flyout`, `WC.flyItem`, `WC.dialog`, `WC.toast`, the `.taskpane` overlay pattern (dialogs.js navPane @258), and the existing `toggleReadAloud` (commands.js @800). View-mode CSS lives in styles/editor.css keyed on `#workarea.view-*` (web-layout block @280 is the template). 1) 100% BUTTON (1 line; FIXES A BUG): The button's cmd is the string "100" (ribbon-data.js id view.zoom.100), but the only handler is `H.zoom100` (commands.js:143), never reached — so it currently shows notImplemented. Add near line 143: `H['100'] = () => E().setZoom(1);` (keep zoom100 or alias). setZoom already updates the status-bar zoom readout. 2) DRAFT VIEW (small): In editor.js `setView` (line 369) add 'view-draft' to the classList.remove(...) list so switching away clears it. Add `H.draft = () => E().setView('draft');` in commands.js View section. Append to editor.css (mirror view-web @280): #workarea.view-draft #editor { box-shadow:none; min-height:auto; width:100%; max-width:850px; padding:24px 48px; } #workarea.view-draft #ruler { display:none; } #workarea.view-draft .pagebreak-guide { border-top:1px dotted #9a9a9a; } #workarea.view-draft .pagebreak-guide::before, #workarea.view-draft .pagebreak-guide::after { display:none; } #workarea.view-draft #editor .wc-header, ... .wc-footer, ... .wc-page-border, ... img, ... .wc-shape, ... .wc-wordart { display:none; } Gives a plain continuous column with dotted page-break markers and no headers/footers/images; Print Layout restores all since the class is removed. 3) OUTLINE VIEW (medium — the substantive feature): A full-window restructure OVERLAY that reads live headings into a collapsible tree; promote/demote/move/expand-collapse act on the real #editor DOM, then dismiss back to print view (avoids re-architecting the contenteditable while giving real behavior). A. `H.outline = () => WC.Outline.open();` in commands.js View section. B. New file src/renderer/js/outline.js (IIFE on window.WC, like other modules). open()/close(): - open(): if open, close. Build overlay `el('div',{class:'outline-view',id:'outline-view'})` appended to #workarea (same parent the taskpanes use) + a slim toolbar mimicking the Outlining tab's Outline Tools group. - Model: walk E().node children in order. H1..H6 = outline node at level=tag number; non-heading blocks attach under the most recent heading as Body Text. Keep each tree row's reference to its source DOM element so edits map back. - Rows: indent by (level-1)*20px; heading rows get a clickable circled +/− symbol, body rows a small dot. Row text editable (contentEditable) writing back to the source element's textContent on blur (keeps it real). - Toolbar ops on the selected/clicked row's source element(s): Promote = retag heading down one number (H3→H2) by replacing element with same-text element of new tag; Promote to Heading 1 = retag H1; Demote = retag up one (max H6); Demote to Body Text = convert to

; Move Up/Down = move the heading element WITH all following siblings up to the next equal-or-higher heading, as a group; Expand/Collapse = toggle a collapsed flag hiding descendant rows (view state); Show Level dropdown (flyout: All + Level 1..9) = set maxLevel, hide deeper rows; Show Text Formatting checkbox = toggle `plain` class (uniform font); Show First Line Only checkbox = toggle `firstline` class (nowrap+ellipsis on body rows); Close button = close() + ensure setView('print'). - After any structural change, re-derive the tree from the DOM and re-render so model never drifts. - On close, #editor already reflects all changes (edited directly) — nothing to sync. C. CSS append: `.outline-view{position:absolute;inset:0;background:#fff;z-index:30;overflow:auto;padding:16px 40px;}` + sticky `.ov-toolbar`, `.ov-row{display:flex;gap:6px}`, `.ov-sym{cursor:pointer;width:16px}`, `.outline-view.plain *{font:11pt Calibri!important;font-weight:400!important}`, first-line clamp class. D. Register `` in index.html. 4) IMMERSIVE READER (medium): Full-window read-only reading overlay cloning the doc text with adjustable column width / page color / line focus / text spacing / syllables / read aloud; reuse the Web Speech read-aloud logic. A. `H.immersiveReader = () => WC.Immersive.open();` B. New file src/renderer/js/immersive.js (IIFE). open()/close(): - open(): overlay `el('div',{class:'immersive',id:'immersive'})` appended to (covers ribbon too, like real Immersive Reader). Toolbar: Close (×) + flyout buttons: Column Width, Page Color, Line Focus, Text Spacing toggle, Syllables toggle, Read Aloud. - Content: clone E().getHTML() into `.im-doc`, contenteditable=false, strip guides; centered measure column. - Column Width (Very Narrow/Narrow/Moderate/Wide) = set .im-doc max-width 360/520/680/900px. - Page Color flyout swatches: white, sepia #f5ecd8, light grey/yellow/green/blue/pink, black-invert (#0c0c0c bg + #eee text); set overlay bg + text color. - Line Focus (None/1/3/5): dim .im-doc and render a clear band of N line-heights tracking the pointer; Up/Down arrows advance the band one line — genuine line-by-line focus. - Text Spacing toggle = class `im-spaced` (word-spacing/letter-spacing/line-height up, wider readable font). - Syllables toggle = class `im-syllables` + JS pass splitting visible words on vowel-group boundaries inserting a thin "·" (heuristic, like Word's own). Re-render from original text on toggle. - Read Aloud = reuse Web Speech utterance from toggleReadAloud; add per-word highlight via SpeechSynthesisUtterance `onboundary` wrapping the current word in `.im-doc`; voice
specs.feasibility:
ALL FOUR ARE FEASIBLE as real single-window/desktop behavior — none require external services. - 100% button: fully feasible, one-line fix. It is currently BROKEN (notImplemented) because the ribbon cmd is the string "100" while the handler is named zoom100 — never connected. Trivial. - Draft view: fully feasible — a pure CSS view mode added to the existing setView system (same mechanism as Web Layout, which already works). Real continuous no-page layout with dotted page-break markers and hidden headers/footers/images. - Outline view: feasible with REAL behavior. Promote/Demote/Move/Expand-Collapse/Show Level operate on the live #editor DOM (retagging headings, reordering blocks) so changes genuinely persist in the document — authentic, not a mockup. Honest simplifications to document: it runs as a full-window overlay over the same contenteditable rather than spawning a separate "Outlining" ribbon tab, and outline editing is row-based rather than the contenteditable itself reflowing — functionally equivalent (real structural edits), a minor UX deviation. - Immersive Reader: feasible. Column width, page color, text spacing, line focus, syllables, and read-aloud (Web Speech API, already used by existing Read Aloud) are all client-side. Honest notes: (a) presents a read-only clone — Word's Immersive Reader is also read-only, so this matches; (b) the Syllables splitter uses an English vowel-group heuristic — Word's own syllabification is also heuristic/imperfect, so this is a faithful approximation; (c) per-word highlight relies on SpeechSynthesisUtterance `onboundary`, supported in Chromium/Electron, so it works; voices come from the OS via getVoices(). NOTHING here is genuinely infeasible. The only honesty notes needed cover two minor UX deviations (Outline as overlay not a separate ribbon tab; Syllables as heuristic). Everything else is true Word-equivalent behavior. Manual in-app verification (Electron; run via the project run skill or `npm start` — no renderer automated-test harness found): 1) 100% button: zoom to another level (One Page), click View > 100% → status-bar reads 100% and #pages transform is scale(1); confirm NO "not implemented" toast (regression for the cmd "100" bug). 2) Draft view: type heading + paragraph + image + header. View > Draft → page shadow/edges gone, single continuous column, header/footer + image hidden, manual page breaks shown as dotted lines, ruler hidden. Print Layout → all restored. Round-trip Web/Read/Draft leaves no stale view-* class on #workarea. 3) Outline view: create H1/H2/H3 + body paragraphs. View > Outline: tree indents per level; body rows show Body Text dot. Collapse an H1 → sub-rows hide; expand → reappear. Show Level=Level 2 → only H1+H2 visible. Select H3, Promote → becomes H2 (verify tree + underlying

after closing). Demote to Body Text → becomes

. Move Down on a heading → it + subordinate paragraphs move below next sibling group (reflected in #editor order after closing). Toggle Show Text Formatting / Show First Line Only. Close → Print Layout with all structural edits persisted. 4) Immersive Reader: View > Immersive Reader: Column Width Very Narrow vs Wide changes measure. Page Color sepia + black-invert apply correct bg/text. Text Spacing widens spacing/line-height. Syllables inserts · in words; untoggle restores clean text. Line Focus=3 dims doc except a 3-line band; arrows move it. Read Aloud plays, highlights current word, voice select + speed slider affect output, Close stops speech and removes overlay. 5) Regression: Read/Print/Web/Focus/Ruler/Gridlines/Nav Pane/Zoom/One Page/Multiple Pages/Page Width still behave (they share setView/setZoom and the workarea class list we extended).

specs.feature: View tab — Page Movement group (Vertical / Side to Side), Window group (New Window, Arrange All, Split, View Side by Side, Synchronous Scrolling, Reset Window Position, Switch Windows), Macros (View Macros / Record Macro = VBA), and the SharePoint Properties button. Defined in ribbon-data.js lines ~2507-2700. All currently fall through to WC.notImplemented because no handlers exist in commands.js (H.vertical, H.sideToSide, H.newWindow, H.arrangeAll, H.split, H.viewSideBySide, H.synchronousScrolling, H.resetWindowPosition, H.switchWindows, H.macros, H.properties are all absent).
specs.wordBehavior:
PAGE MOVEMENT (mutually-exclusive toggles, Print Layout only): Vertical = default; pages flow top-to-bottom, normal vertical scroll, zoom enabled. Side to Side = pages flip horizontally like a book; you move with the horizontal scrollbar, mouse wheel, or PageUp/PageDown, one/two pages at a time; zoom is DISABLED (the Zoom group is replaced by a single "Thumbnails" command that shows page tiles to jump to a page); only works in Print Layout — switching to Web/Read reverts to Vertical.

WINDOW GROUP: New Window = opens a SECOND OS window showing the SAME document (titled "Doc1:2"); edits in either reflect in both because it is the same doc. Arrange All = tiles all open Word windows so they're all visible at once. Split = splits the CURRENT window into two horizontal panes viewing two parts of the SAME document; each pane has its own scrollbar/ruler and scrolls independently; drag the split bar to resize; double-click the bar (or View > Remove Split) removes it. View Side by Side = places two open documents next to each other to compare; requires >=2 open docs. Synchronous Scrolling = sub-toggle of Side by Side; when on, scrolling one scrolls the other in lock-step (default ON when Side by Side starts). Reset Window Position = re-tiles the two side-by-side windows to share the screen 50/50. Switch Windows = dropdown listing every open document window; click to focus it; a check marks the current one.

MACROS (split button): View Macros (Alt+F8) opens the Macros dialog (list of macros + Run/Edit/Create/Delete/Step Into); Edit/Create opens the VBA editor (Visual Basic for Applications). Record Macro opens a dialog (macro name, store-in, assign to button/keyboard) then records UI actions into VBA. Pause/Resume Recording toggles capture. This is a full VBA/COM scripting engine.

SHAREPOINT PROPERTIES: This group only appears when the file lives in a SharePoint library; the Properties button shows the Document Information Panel / server library columns (Title, Author, managed metadata, content type) synced to SharePoint. Separately, Word's general Document Properties (File > Info > Properties > Advanced Properties) has Summary (Title, Subject, Author, Manager, Company, Category, Keywords/Tags, Comments) and Statistics (pages, words, characters, paragraphs, lines, created/modified, editing time) tabs.
specs.webFindings: Microsoft Support and trade sources confirm the exact behaviors. Side to Side: "Side to Side only applies in Print Layout view… any other View it's back to Vertical"; "switches off the ability to pick a zoom setting"; navigate via horizontal scrollbar, mouse wheel, or PageUp/PageDown; activating it replaces the Zoom group with a single "Thumbnails" command (sources: support.microsoft.com view-a-page-at-a-time-side-by-side, office-watch.com/2023/side-to-side-view-word-documents, officesmart.wordpress.com page-movement). Split: View > Window > Split; "only… two parts of the same document," each pane has own ruler/scrollbars, drag the dividing line to resize, double-click the bar or "Remove Split" to clear, Synchronous Scrolling turns the lock-step off (bettersolutions.com word/documents/split-screens, wordribbon.tips.net T007550, support.microsoft.com view-two-parts). Window group: New Window opens a second window of the same doc; Arrange All tiles all windows; View Side by Side + Synchronous Scrolling compare two docs in lock-step; Switch Windows lists open docs; F6 jumps between split panes (support.microsoft.com view-files-in-split-and-multiple-windows, thewindowsclub.com). Macros: View > Macros > View Macros / Record Macro, Edit opens the VBA editor (support.microsoft.com create-or-run-a-macro, bettersolutions.com developer-tab). Document Properties: File > Info > Properties > Advanced Properties with Summary + Statistics tabs (support.microsoft.com view-or-change-the-properties, howtogeek.com 216295).
specs.implementationPlan:
Add handlers in commands.js (H.*), CSS in editor.css, a couple of helpers in editor.js, plus a Properties dialog in dialogs.js. DOM facts that drive the design: single continuous #editor inside #pages inside scrollable #canvas; pagination is VIRTUAL (pageH=1056 in print/816 landscape; guides drawn as overlays, no discrete .page elements). So page-flipping works by snapping #canvas scrollLeft/Top to multiples of pageH*zoom, not by iterating page elements.

PAGE MOVEMENT (REAL):
- Track WC.Editor.pageMovement ('vertical'|'sideToSide'); default 'vertical'.
- H.vertical = () => setPageMovement('vertical'); H.sideToSide = () => setPageMovement('sideToSide').
- Add Editor.setPageMovement(mode): only allow 'sideToSide' when view==='print' (else toast "Side to Side only works in Print Layout" and stay vertical). Add class 'side-to-side' to #workarea. In setView(), if leaving 'print', force pageMovement back to 'vertical'.
- CSS: #workarea.side-to-side #canvas { overflow-x: auto; overflow-y: hidden; } #workarea.side-to-side #pages { flex-direction: row; } — but because content is one continuous #editor (can't be split into row flex children), implement flipping instead via scroll-snap on #canvas using CSS scroll-snap plus invisible snap anchors: in repaginate(), when side-to-side is on, additionally lay out the editor as fixed-height page columns. Simpler robust approach that fits the virtual model: keep vertical DOM but intercept wheel — on .side-to-side, a wheel handler on #canvas does e.preventDefault() and scrolls vertically by exactly one pageH*zoom per notch (snap to nearest page), giving the book-flip feel; PageUp/PageDown already move ~one screen, refine to one page. Mark Vertical/Side toggles checked (markChecked pattern). Disable zoom: when side-to-side, set a flag; H.zoom/onePage/multiplePages/pageWidth/zoom100 early-return with a toast "Zoom is unavailable in Side to Side; switch to Vertical."; the status-bar zoom can be greyed (optional). Add a "Thumbnails" affordance: reuse navPane-style overlay listing page numbers that scrollIntoView the page offset. This is REAL and single-window-feasible.

WINDOW GROUP:
- Split (REAL, the marquee feature): H.split toggles a draggable horizontal split of the CURRENT window. Build Editor.splitView: create a #split-bar (absolutely positioned over #canvas) and a second read-only pane #editor-mirror that is a clone of #editor (contentEditable=false, pointer scroll only) shown in the bottom half; the live #editor occupies the top half (clip its visible region with a wrapper that has overflow + its own scrollTop). Both panes scroll the SAME content independently. Keep the mirror in sync: on Editor input/repaginate, copy this.node.innerHTML into the mirror (debounced; strip guides). Drag #split-bar (mousedown/mousemove on document) to resize the two panes; double-click the bar removes the split; H.split called again removes it. This satisfies "two scroll positions of the same document, draggable split." Add CSS for .wc-split-top/.wc-split-bottom/.wc-split-bar (cursor: row-resize). This is fully feasible in one window.
- New Window (REAL via Electron, with honest scope note): add IPC. In preload.js add window.newWindow: () => ipcRenderer.invoke('window:new'). In main.js add ipcMain.handle('window:new') that calls createWindow() (refactor createWindow to return the window, push to a windows[] array, and on ready-to-show optionally pass the current doc HTML so the new window opens the same content). H.newWindow = async () => { await window.wordAPI.window.new(); }. Because the clone has no shared doc model across processes, the new window opens a fresh copy of the current document (send current HTML via a query/IPC handshake) — note in tooltip/toast that live co-editing across windows isn't wired (each is independent after open). This is a REAL second BrowserWindow.
- Switch Windows (REAL, partial): main tracks windows[] with titles; add ipcMain.handle('window:list') -> [{id,title}] and 'window:focus' (id) -> win.focus(); H.switchWindows opens a flyout listing them with a check on the focused one. Feasible once New Window exists.
- Arrange All (REAL, partial): ipcMain.handle('window:arrangeAll') tiles all BrowserWindows using screen.getPrimaryDisplay().workArea (split width by count). H.arrangeAll calls it. Feasible.
- View Side by Side / Synchronous Scrolling / Reset Window Position (HONEST STUB): these compare TWO DIFFERENT open documents in lock-step. The clone is effectively single-document (no multi-doc model; New Window opens a copy, not a registry of distinct docs). Implement as toasts pointing to docs/NOT_IMPLEMENTED.md, OR a partial: if >=2 windows exist, arrange the two side by side (reuse arrangeAll for 2) and toast that synchronous cross-window scrolling needs a shared doc model. Recommend: arrange-2 for View Side by Side + Reset Window Position; toast-stub Synchronous Scrolling (cross-process scroll mirroring not wired). Mark feasible:"partial".

MACROS (HONEST INFEASIBLE STUB): H.macros split-button. Dropdown items View Macros / Record Macro… / Pause Recording. Each shows WC.toast/dialog: "Macros run on Visual Basic for Applications (VBA), a COM scripting engine Word embeds. This clone has no VBA runtime, so macros can't be recorded or executed. See docs/NOT_IMPLEMENTED.md." Show a realistic but inert Macros dialog (empty macro list + disabled Run/Edit/Create) so the UI is faithful. Mark feasible:"no".

SHAREPOINT / DOCUMENT PROPERTIES: H.properties (REAL Document Properties dialog, the genuinely-feasible part): WC.Dialogs.documentProperties() — a dialog with Summary fields (Title prefilled from document.title/.title-center, Author defaulting to the OS user, Subject, Manager, Company, Category, Keywords/Tags, Comments) editable and stored on WC.Editor.docProps (persist into saved HTML metadata if a meta slot exists; otherwise keep in-session), and a Statistics section reusing E().counts()/pageCount()/paragraph & line counts exactly like D.wordCount (lines 226-235). This is fully feasible. BUT the *SharePoint sync* part (server library columns, Document Information Panel bound to a SharePoint list, managed metadata) is infeasible — add a banner/toast in the dialog: "SharePoint library properties require a SharePoint server connection — not available in this local clone (see docs/NOT_IMPLEMENTED.md). Showing local Document Properties instead." Mark the SharePoint control feasible:"no" but deliver the real local Properties dialog behind it.

WIRING: register every new H.* in the Commands.run path (they're auto-dispatched by cmd id). For split-button 'macros', also handle in Commands.dropdown (add `if (cmd==='macros') return macrosMenu(node);`). Add CSS to editor.css for .side-to-side, .wc-split-*, thumbnails strip. Update ribbon-data feasibility flags where behavior is now real (vertical/sideToSide/split/newWindow/switchWindows/arrangeAll -> keep "partial"/"yes"; properties stays "no" but opens real dialog). Add concise entries to docs/NOT_IMPLEMENTED.md for VBA macros, cross-document View Side by Side / Synchronous Scrolling, and SharePoint server properties.
specs.feasibility:
FEASIBLE / REAL: (1) Page Movement Vertical<->Side to Side as wheel/PageUp-Down page-snapping on #canvas with zoom disabled + a Thumbnails jump strip, Print-Layout-gated — fully buildable on the existing virtual-pagination model. (2) Split = draggable horizontal split of the current window into two independently-scrolling panes of the SAME doc (live #editor on top, synced read-only mirror clone on bottom, draggable/double-click-to-remove bar) — fully buildable, single window. (3) New Window = a real second Electron BrowserWindow opening a copy of the current document via new IPC (window:new) — real, though the two windows are independent copies (no live cross-window co-editing). (4) Arrange All + Switch Windows = real once multiple BrowserWindows exist, via window:list/window:focus/window:arrangeAll IPC tiling with screen.workArea. (5) Document Properties dialog (Summary + Statistics) behind the SharePoint Properties button — fully buildable, reuses E().counts().

PARTIAL: View Side by Side / Synchronous Scrolling / Reset Window Position — the genuine feature compares two DIFFERENT documents in lock-step across windows; the clone has no shared multi-document model or cross-process scroll bus, so deliver an arrange-two-windows approximation + honest toast that synchronous cross-window scrolling isn't wired.

GENUINELY INFEASIBLE (honest stubs, documented in docs/NOT_IMPLEMENTED.md): (a) Macros / Record Macro — needs the VBA (Visual Basic for Applications) COM scripting runtime Word embeds; no JS equivalent, so an inert-but-faithful Macros dialog + clear toast. (b) SharePoint library Properties / Document Information Panel — needs a live SharePoint server + managed-metadata service; replaced by the local Document Properties dialog with a banner explaining the SharePoint sync is unavailable. (c) True New Window live co-editing (same in-memory doc across OS windows) — Electron multi-window works but a shared CRDT/doc model is out of scope; windows open independent copies.
specs.testPlan:
MANUAL (run the Electron app, View tab): 1) Page Movement: click Side to Side — verify it only engages in Print Layout (try from Web/Read: should toast and stay vertical), verify zoom buttons now toast "unavailable", scroll wheel/PageDown advances one page at a time, Thumbnails strip jumps to a page; click Vertical — normal scroll + zoom restored; switching to Web Layout auto-reverts to vertical. 2) Split: click Split — window divides into two panes; scroll each independently to different pages of the same doc; type in the top (live) pane and confirm the bottom mirror updates; drag the bar to resize; double-click the bar (and re-click Split) to remove. 3) New Window: click New Window — a second OS window opens with the same content; confirm both are editable. 4) Switch Windows: dropdown lists both windows with a check on current; clicking focuses the other. 5) Arrange All: both windows tile to share the screen. 6) View Side by Side / Sync Scrolling / Reset Position: with 2 windows, verify the arrange-two approximation and the honest toast for sync scrolling. 7) Macros split: View Macros shows the inert dialog; Record Macro/Pause show the VBA-infeasible toast. 8) SharePoint Properties: opens the Document Properties dialog — edit Title/Author/etc., confirm Statistics (words/pages/paragraphs) match Review > Word Count, and the SharePoint-unavailable banner shows.

AUTOMATED/SMOKE: reuse the existing --shot screenshot harness (main.js maybeScreenshot / executeJavaScript) to script WC.Commands.run for sideToSide, split, and properties and capture PNGs; assert no console errors and that document.querySelector('.wc-split-bar')/#editor-mirror exist after split, #workarea.side-to-side class toggles, and the Properties dialog renders. Verify the build still launches with `npm start` and the View ribbon controls no longer hit WC.notImplemented for the implemented cmds.
